The **Archdiocese of Guangzhou[*[clarify](/wiki/Wikipedia:Please_clarify "Wikipedia:Please clarify")*] (Canton)** ([Latin](/wiki/Latin_language "Latin language"): *Archidioecesis Cantonensis*, [Chinese](/wiki/Traditional_Chinese_characters "Traditional Chinese characters"): 天主教廣州總教區) is an [archdiocese](/wiki/Archdiocese "Archdiocese") located in the city of [Guangzhou](/wiki/Guangzhou "Guangzhou") in China.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Archdiocese_of_Guangzhou&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

* 1848: Established as Apostolic Vicariate of Guangdong-Guangxi from the [Diocese of Macau](/wiki/Diocese_of_Macau "Diocese of Macau")
* 1875: Renamed as Apostolic Vicariate of Guangdong
* April 6, 1914: Renamed as Apostolic Vicariate of Guangzhou[*[clarify](/wiki/Wikipedia:Please_clarify "Wikipedia:Please clarify")*]
* April 11, 1946: Promoted as Metropolitan Archdiocese of Guangzhou[*[clarify](/wiki/Wikipedia:Please_clarify "Wikipedia:Please clarify")*]

The Apostolic Administrator of the Archdiocese of Guangzhou,[*[clarify](/wiki/Wikipedia:Please_clarify "Wikipedia:Please clarify")*] Bishop [Dominic Deng Yi-ming, S.J.](/wiki/Dominic_Tang "Dominic Tang")[*[clarify](/wiki/Wikipedia:Please_clarify "Wikipedia:Please clarify")*] was imprisoned in 1958. His release in 1981 due to a necessary cancer treatment in Hong Kong and his subsequent appointment to archbishop caused the Chinese authorities respond with the appointment of Joseph Ye Yinyun. Archbishop Ye was never recognised by the Holy See, his successor Archbishop James [Lin Bingliang](/wiki/Lin_Bingliang "Lin Bingliang") was recognised in 1998 only after Archbishop Deng's death in 1995. Thus the rift between the underground church and the Patriotic Church came for the Archdiocese to an end. The present archbishop Joseph Gan Junqiu won papal consent even before the consent of the Chinese authorities. His ordination was delayed, because the Chinese authorities originally wanted to have two illicit bishops preside over it.

## Leadership

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Archdiocese_of_Guangzhou&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Leadership")]

* [Archbishops](/wiki/Archbishop "Archbishop") of Guangzhou (Roman rite)
  + Archbishop [Joseph Gan Junqiu](/w/index.php?title=Joseph_Gan_Junqiu&action=edit&redlink=1 "Joseph Gan Junqiu (page does not exist)") (甘俊邱, consecration on Dec 4, 2007, after prior papal approval)
  + Archbishop [James Lin Bingliang](/w/index.php?title=James_Lin_Bingliang&action=edit&redlink=1 "James Lin Bingliang (page does not exist)") (林秉良, 1990-2001, papal approval in 1998).
  + Archbishop [Joseph Ye Yinyun](/w/index.php?title=Joseph_Ye_Yinyun&action=edit&redlink=1 "Joseph Ye Yinyun (page does not exist)") (叶荫芸, 1981-1990, without papal approval)
  + Archbishop [Dominic Deng Yi-ming](/wiki/Dominic_Deng_Yi-ming "Dominic Deng Yi-ming") (Tang Yee-ming), [S.J.](/wiki/Society_of_Jesus "Society of Jesus") (鄧以明) (May 26, 1981 – June 27, 1995, after 1981 in exile)
  + Bishop Dominic Deng Yi-ming (Tang Yee-ming), S.J. (鄧以明) (later Archbishop) (Apostolic Administrator October 1, 1950 – May 26, 1981)
  + Archbishop [Antoine-Pierre-Jean Fourquet](/w/index.php?title=Antoine-Pierre-Jean_Fourquet&action=edit&redlink=1 "Antoine-Pierre-Jean Fourquet (page does not exist)"), [M.E.P.](/wiki/M.E.P. "M.E.P.") (April 11, 1946 – December 11, 1947)
* Vicars Apostolic of Guangzhou 廣州 (Roman Rite)
  + Bishop Antoine-Pierre-Jean Fourquet, M.E.P. (later Archbishop) (February 20, 1923 – April 11, 1946)
  + Bishop [Jean-Baptiste-Marie Budes de Guébriant](/w/index.php?title=Jean-Baptiste-Marie_Budes_de_Gu%C3%A9briant&action=edit&redlink=1 "Jean-Baptiste-Marie Budes de Guébriant (page does not exist)"), M.E.P. (later Archbishop) (April 28, 1916 – March 21, 1921)
  + Bishop [Adolphe Rayssac](/w/index.php?title=Adolphe_Rayssac&action=edit&redlink=1 "Adolphe Rayssac (page does not exist)"), M.E.P. (Apostolic Administrator 1915 – 1916)
  + Bishop [Jean-Marie Mérel](/w/index.php?title=Jean-Marie_M%C3%A9rel&action=edit&redlink=1 "Jean-Marie Mérel (page does not exist)"), M.E.P. (April 6, 1914 – August 6, 1914)
* Vicars Apostolic of Guangdong 廣東 (Roman Rite)
  + Bishop Jean-Marie Mérel, M.E.P. (April 20, 1901 – April 6, 1914)
  + Bishop [Augustin Chausse](/w/index.php?title=Augustin_Chausse&action=edit&redlink=1 "Augustin Chausse (page does not exist)"), M.E.P. (April 5, 1886 – October 12, 1900)
  + Bishop [Philippe François Zéphirin Guillemin](/wiki/Philippe_Fran%C3%A7ois_Z%C3%A9phirin_Guillemin "Philippe François Zéphirin Guillemin"), M.E.P. (November 16, 1853 – April 5, 1886)

## Catholic churches

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Archdiocese_of_Guangzhou&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Catholic churches")]

There are several catholic churches in Guangzhou city. Few of them are offering masses in foreign languages regularly.

**Guangzhou City**

* [Sacred Heart Cathedral of Guangzhou](/wiki/Sacred_Heart_Cathedral_of_Guangzhou "Sacred Heart Cathedral of Guangzhou")
  + Address: No. 56, Yide Road, Guangzhou (一德路56号).
  + English mass: every Sunday 03:30 pm.
* [Shamian](/wiki/Shamian "Shamian") Church of Our Lady of Lourdes [![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f2/Our_Lady_of_Lourdes_Chapel.jpg/250px-Our_Lady_of_Lourdes_Chapel.jpg)](/wiki/File:Our_Lady_of_Lourdes_Chapel.jpg)

  Our Lady of Lourdes Chapel on [Shamian](/wiki/Shamian "Shamian") Island

  + Address: No. 14, Shamian Avenue, [Shamian](/wiki/Shamian "Shamian"), Guangzhou.
  + English mass: every Sunday at 11:00 am.
* St. Francis of Assisi Chapel
  + Address: No. 26, Shanhehou Street, Yuexiu District, Guangzhou (广州市越秀区山河后街26号)
  + English mass: every Sunday at 10:00 am.[*[needs update](/wiki/Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Dates_and_numbers#Chronological_items "Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Dates and numbers")*]
